message:
fix(terminal): improve alternate screen initialization and size detection

Address two terminal compatibility issues:

1. Alternate screen buffer clearing:
   Some terminals (including WezTerm) may show stale content in the
   alternate screen buffer without an explicit clear. Now we:
   - Enter alternate screen
   - Clear the entire buffer
   - Position cursor at (0,0)
   This ensures a clean slate regardless of terminal implementation.

2. Terminal size detection robustness:
   Some terminals briefly report 1x1 dimensions during startup before
   the actual size is available. The size() method now:
   - Returns immediately if dimensions are reasonable (>1x1)
   - Falls back to COLUMNS/LINES environment variables if available
   - Re-probes after a short delay (10ms) as a last resort
   - Returns the original values if all else fails

This improves reliability when launching TUI applications, especially
in environments with slow terminal initialization or unusual terminal
emulators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
